submission_methods.reasoning: All sources consistently show portal as preferred method (Availity), fax as secondary (though number changed), and phone for status inquiries only. Phone transcript confirms 'Availity is the preferred method now' and 'Phone inquiries regarding PA status' from manual.

fax_number.reasoning: Phone transcript (2026-03-22) explicitly states 'That fax number was decommissioned as of February 1st, 2026' referring to old number. Denial letter (2026-03-10) confirms 'this fax line is no longer active for infusion medication prior authorizations as of February 2026'. Both recent sources provide new number (888) 267-3300.

superseded_values.reason: Decommissioned February 1, 2026 per phone rep and denial letter

portal_url.reasoning: Three sources provide full URL www.availity.com. Phone transcript mentions 'Availity' without full URL but references same portal. All sources consistently point to Availity as the portal.

phone_status_only.reasoning: Provider manual and denial letter both specify full IVR path 'Option 3, then Option 1' or 'Option 3, Option 1'. Web page shows incomplete path 'Option 3' only. Phone transcript confirms this number works for PA inquiries.

phone_urgent.reasoning: No source mentions a dedicated urgent phone line. All sources indicate urgent requests go through same submission channels (portal/fax) but with 24-hour turnaround commitment.

phone_hours.reasoning: No source provides specific operating hours for the phone line. This information is missing from all sources.

pa_form.reasoning: Provider manual (2025-01-15) shows AET-PA-2025 while web page (2024-10-01) shows AET-PA-2024. Form version with year 2025 supersedes 2024 version. Manual is more recent and would have updated form version.

superseded_values.reason: Superseded by newer form version AET-PA-2025

selected_value: Completed Aetna Prior Authorization Request Form
Letter of medical necessity from prescribing physician
Step therapy compliance documentation (for applicable biologics)
required_documents.reasoning: Provider manual provides most comprehensive list. Other sources mention similar requirements with consistent core documents across all sources.

sources_considered.value: PA form, chart notes, labs, letter, step therapy docs

chart_note_window_days.reasoning: Phone rep explicitly states 'It's 90 days now. That was updated in the February policy revision. The manual might not reflect that yet.' Denial letter confirms 'exceeds the 90-day documentation window per our updated policy (effective February 1, 2026)'. Both recent sources (phone + denial) agree on 90 days and explicitly reference policy change.

superseded_values.reason: Updated to 90 days in February 2026 policy revision per phone rep and denial letter

lab_window_days.reasoning: Provider manual specifies 'Lab results supporting medical necessity (within 90 days)'. No other source conflicts with this timeframe.

turnaround_standard_days.reasoning: All sources consistently state 5 business days as the standard policy turnaround. Phone transcript notes operational delays ('7-10 days' for fax) but confirms policy remains '5 business days once we have everything'.

turnaround_portal_days.reasoning: Phone transcript confirms 'Availity submissions are still on track at 5 days'. Web page shows '3-5 business days' average. Phone rep indicates portal maintains standard turnaround unlike fax delays.

turnaround_fax_days.reasoning: Phone transcript states 'fax submissions are taking 7-10 business days right now because of the transition'. This reflects current operational reality due to fax number change. Web page shows outdated '5-7 business days' from before transition.

superseded_values.reason: Outdated due to fax system transition causing delays per phone rep

turnaround_urgent_hours.reasoning: All three sources that mention urgent turnaround consistently state 24 hours. Phone transcript confirms 'Urgent is still 24 hours either way' despite other delays.

pend_period_days.reasoning: Provider manual mentions 'Incomplete submissions will receive a request for additional information within 2 business days' but does not specify auto-denial deadline. This field is not clearly stated in any source.

appeal_fax.reasoning: Denial letter provides specific appeals fax number (888) 267-3350. Web page shows (888) 267-3277 which is the old general PA fax that was decommissioned. Denial letter is more recent and authoritative for appeals process.

superseded_values.reason: Web page shows old general PA fax number, denial letter provides dedicated appeals fax

appeal_phone.reasoning: Provider manual and denial letter both specify full IVR path 'Option 3, then Option 1' or 'Option 3, Option 1'. Web page shows incomplete path. Most recent sources provide complete navigation.

appeal_deadline_days.reasoning: Denial letter explicitly states 'Appeals must be submitted within 60 days of this determination'. This is the only source providing this information and comes from official determination letter.

appeal_mail.reasoning: No source provides a mailing address for written appeals. All sources mention fax and portal options only.

system_migration_in_progress.reasoning: Phone transcript indicates 'fax submissions are taking 7-10 business days right now because of the transition'. Denial letter references fax line change 'as of February 2026'. Evidence points to system transition affecting operations.

common_denial_reasons.reasoning: No source provides statistics on common denial reasons. Denial letter shows examples (wrong fax, outdated documentation) but no percentages.

coverage_states.reasoning: No source specifies which states this Aetna policy covers. Information not provided in any source.

step_therapy_required.reasoning: Provider manual states 'For Remicade and Entyvio: member must have documented trial and failure of at least one conventional therapy'. Phone transcript confirms 'must try and fail conventional therapy first'. Web page states 'Requires step therapy documentation'.

prior_treatment_failure_required.reasoning: Provider manual explicitly states 'Documentation of failure on conventional therapy required'. Phone transcript confirms 'must try and fail conventional therapy first'.

biosimilar_requirement.reasoning: No source mentions biosimilar requirements for Remicade. Provider manual states 'No mention of biosimilar requirements'.

preferred_biosimilars.reasoning: No source mentions specific biosimilars for Remicade.

auth_period_months.reasoning: Provider manual and web page both state initial auth period of 6 months. Provider manual provides more detail with 'Initial auth: 6 months. Renewal: 12 months.'

auth_period_initial_months.reasoning: Provider manual explicitly states 'Initial auth: 6 months'.

specialist_required.reasoning: Provider manual states 'Diagnosis must be confirmed by specialist'. This is the only source addressing specialist requirements for Remicade.

specific_testing.reasoning: No source mentions specific lab testing requirements for Remicade (unlike Keytruda with PD-L1 or Rituxan with CD20+).

indication_specific_requirements.reasoning: No source provides indication-specific requirements for Remicade. Requirements appear to apply broadly.

diagnosis_restrictions.reasoning: No source specifies allowed diagnoses for Remicade coverage.
